content:
Hello all.

Is anyone else interested in hosting the SIG-Embedded meetings, and chairing the discusions?

It has been my pleasure, and a privillage to setup and help run the SIG-Embedded meetings. Along the way, we've had awesome contributions and conversations with folks from Siemens (of course), Bosch, Schneider Electric, Aptiv, Woven (Toyota), Collins Aerospace (part of RTX), Amazon, Intel, Midokura (Sony) and Xiaomi.

All this was done with the amazing support of the Byetcode Alliance - a big thank you to the TSC and the board for helping us set this all up.

There was a SIG-Embedded meeting today, and I think this all just fell through the cracks. -- Siemens is no longer a member of the Bytecode Alliance. This means that I no longer have access to the tools required to host the Embedded Special Interest Group (Zoom, Youtube for uploads, Git for meeting notes etc).

The WALI SIG-Embedded would need a new chair to continue the discussions. If you are interested in taking up this role, please do reach out to David Bryant, or Oscar Spencer at the Bytecode Alliance. I am sure they would be more than happy to help. It would be awesome if we could keep the Embedded SIG going, and of course Siemens would be happy to contribute to a future SIG, assuming we can find a new chair and host.

Siemens is still an active community member in Wasm and will remain part of the W3C and will participate in the discussions on WebAssembly, and will work with the WAMR team and the WAMR runtime.

All the best,
Chris

Dear Chris and hello all,

sorry for the late reply. When the TSC revisited the SIGs two weeks ago I asked about the embedded SIG and voiced that I would love to see the SIG continue its meetings.

I received backing from my employer to step up as an intermittent chair until a full chair is nominated and the SIG has re-established its meeting cadence. I will open tomorrow's meeting and start the discussion and news-exchange about the component model on embedded.

Looking forward to talk to you tomorrow in the next Europe and Asia meeting at 2026-06-09T10:00:00+02:00.

Yours, Christof

PS: I can't offer a solution for chairing the US+Asia meetings, let's see tomorrow whether we can come up with something.
I uploaded the recording of Jun 9th at https://youtu.be/wIt0byr0h00 . If you want to learn about difficulties with the component model on embedded and potential upcoming solutions take a watch.

Unfortunately I didn't manage in time to find a person running the America & Asia meeting this week, but I plan to open the next meeting at the normal time 2026-07-07T08:00:00+02:00 . Please feel free to come up with suggestions for an agenda.

I am confused, today's America & Europe meeting should be at 2026-07-07T17:00:00+02:00, shouldn't it? At least one of my calendars shows it this way.

I plan to enable today's meeting but I have a doctor's appointment with my mother before and can't reliably plan to be at my computer on time.

Sorry, Pilot Error, I was unable to find the recurring meeting in zoom on my Android phone, now I know where to look and can start it even when not at the computer. Also at the time the meeting started I was driving a car, so I was unable to experiment further. I will try again...
